active_phases.w1_t0_completion: 2026-04-16 @ 9b21112 — goose availability spike via RuFlo swarm-1776331122253 (researcher agent direct). Outcome B primary (subprocess-via-ACP/MCP); git-dep P2 fallback. Q#1 closed: block/goose NOT on crates.io (`goose` crate there is Tag1 HTTP load-tester unrelated); workspace `publish=false` blocks direct cargo dep; `goose-sdk` is ACP client not embeddable library. Q#4 closed: `ToolInspector` trait in `crates/goose/src/tool_inspection.rs` (Allow/Deny/RequireApproval semantics = PreToolUse) reachable via `ToolInspectionManager::add_inspector(Box<dyn ToolInspector>)` but ONLY via git-dep; ACP/MCP surface has NO equivalent interface. Hook injection strategy = wrapping MCP middleware `eaasp-scoped-hook-mcp` intercepting `tools/call` per ADR-V2-006 §2/§3 envelope, dispatching bash hooks, mirroring Allow/Deny/InjectAndContinue, forwarding to real tool server. Fallback (if emulation drift): flip to git-dep `goose = { git = 'https://github.com/block/goose', tag = 'v1.31.0', default-features = false, features = ['rustls-tls'] }` → `agent.tool_inspection_manager_mut().add_inspector(Box::new(EaaspScopedHookInspector))`. Scope amendments cascade: T1 drops goose cargo dep (scaffold = tonic/tokio/prost/anyhow/tracing + proto only); T2 pivots to subprocess Child + ACP client per session; T3 GROWS +1 day (16 gRPC methods PLUS new eaasp-scoped-hook-mcp middleware crate); T4 contract needs GOOSE_BIN env + middleware child lifecycle; T5 E2E verifies subprocess+middleware stack. §9 Q#1+Q#4 replaced with ✅ CLOSED Chinese findings; §3.3 diagram line 143 fallback callout re-read as primary/fallback axis swap. Open items F1 (extensions config middleware insertion pre-T2), F3 (no ACP cancellation → SIGTERM+kill fallback), F4 (middleware crate location — recommend own crate for W2 reuse). Memory saved: project_s1_w1_t0_goose_spike.md.

S3.T4 (Stop hooks extension claude-code style — stop_hooks.rs 261 LOC + stop_hooks_integration.rs 404 LOC + harness.rs/loop_config.rs/mod.rs surgical mods; 5 files 747 insertions; StopHookDecision::{Noop, InjectAndContinue} + dispatch 'first decisive verdict' + MAX_STOP_HOOK_INJECTIONS=3 cap; death-spiral prevention across 14 error return paths watertight; reviewer M2 inline-fixed M1+N1→D114/D115/D116; 4+4 PASS + 11 regressions PASS @ a209716)

S3.T5 (Scoped-hook executor — ADR-V2-006 envelope contract + Rust G1 substitute_hook_vars wiring + Rust G7 ScopedStopHookBridge + Python ScopedCommandExecutor subprocess path + Python substitution wiring + 10 new tests. 9 files changed (+1700 LOC across ADR/Rust-harness/Rust-bridge/Python-executor/Python-service/4-rust-integration-tests/6-python-integration-tests). Rust: 107+2323 PASS, 0 regressions. Python: 104+6 PASS, 0 new regressions (1 pre-existing D110). Scope: D50 deferred→D117, D51+D53 closed. Reviewer APPROVE-WITH-COMMENTS 0 Criticals, 3 Majors all deferred (D120 cross-runtime envelope parity / D121 stop-hook additive / D122 hook_id drift) + N1 inline-fixed. Key design lessons: (1) Rust execute_command + HookContext predates ADR-V2-006 and omits event/skill_id/* fields → D120 catchup for Phase 2.5 goose cert; (2) Python dispatch helpers extracted per-event to avoid 3x duplication; (3) DashMap for session_stop_hooks matches existing AgentRuntime concurrency pattern; (4) build_and_spawn_executor_filtered is sync → DashMap is the right primitive vs tokio::sync::RwLock. RuFlo swarm-1776243400662 hierarchical: scout (Explore) blueprint /tmp/s3t5-blueprint.md 698 lines → T5.A-E sequenced coders → reviewer APPROVE. Plan 19/23)
